details.formula: Highest level of self-reported evidence cited in impact metrics

details.calculation: No quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline

details.calculation: Financials with income breakdown are missing.
Mixed portfolio with minority focus on high-neglectedness areas (Farmed Animals: 11%, Wild Animals: 1%, Companion Animals: 88%, Unspecified: 0%).
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

details.calculation: No disclosure of unintended negative impacts found. This is the industry norm.
details.elaboration: This check rewards epistemic humility. Non-disclosure is standard and not a failure.
details.formula: Check for self-reported unintended consequences or failures.

details.calculation: Organisation provided euthanasia or live-release statistics.
details.formula: For direct rescue orgs, check for euthanasia/live-release rate disclosure.

Youth Organization in Defense of Animals (YODA)

metrics.context_qualifier: Animals medically rehabilitated at the shelter in 2020.

source.quote: 18,254 animals were medically rehabilitated at our shelter in 2020.

metrics.context_qualifier: Success stories of animals being rehomed or rehabilitated and released in 2020.
metrics.metric_name: Animals Rehomed or Rehabilitated and Released

source.quote: Out of those, we have had approx. 17,800 success stories of animals being rehomed or rehabilitated and release.
In 2020, we have vaccinated 8000 animals and Sterilized 1,822 homeless animals
In 2020, we have successfully homed 2489 animals
In 2020, we have vaccinated 8000 animals and Sterilized 1,822 homeless animals

metrics.context_qualifier: Over 5 Lakh meals provided to stray animals during the pandemic lockdown in 2020.

source.quote: With your support, in 2020, we made immense progress in scaling up our sick and injured stray animal rehabilitation practice, rescued a large number of animals who were the victims of grave cruelty, and we scaled up our feeding program to cover over 5 Lakh meals for stray animals who were left starving on the road due to the pandemic.

metrics.context_qualifier: Retired police dogs from explosive and drug detection teams successfully rehabilitated and rehomed.
metrics.metric_name: Retired Police Dogs Rehabilitated and Rehomed

source.quote: 50 Retired Police dogs from explosive and drug detection teams were successfully rehabilitated and rehomed
We organized a robust emergency daily feeding program for thousands of street animals around Maharashtra who were left to fend for themselves during public lockdown periods.

significant_events.summary: Organized a robust daily feeding program for thousands of street animals across Maharashtra during public lockdown periods, providing over 5 Lakh meals.

source.quote: With your support, in 2020, we made immense progress in scaling up our sick and injured stray animal rehabilitation practice, rescued a large number of animals who were the victims of grave cruelty, and we scaled up our feeding program to cover over 5 Lakh meals for stray animals who were left starving on the road due to the pandemic.

significant_events.summary: Scaled up sick and injured stray animal rehabilitation practice, rescuing a large number of animals who were victims of grave cruelty.

source.quote: So we at YODA set up a large scale search and rescue team to go out and identify such animals in pain to treat on site or admit depending upon situation.

significant_events.summary: Set up a large scale search and rescue team to identify and treat sick/injured animals on site or admit them to shelter during lockdown.

source.quote: With more individuals working from home, YODA has a good opportunity to carry out large scale fostering programs.

significant_events.summary: Carried out large scale fostering programs with four times the usual number of individuals fostering orphaned, injured, or sick animals.

source.quote: With empty roads, we were able to reach further and spread ourselves to areas we had never covered previously for our vaccination and sterilisation program.

significant_events.summary: Reached further and spread to new areas for vaccination and sterilization programs due to empty roads during lockdown.

source.quote: YODA tied up with local police in Maharashtra and Bangalore to take in all retired police dogs for behavioral rehabilitation and adoption.

significant_events.summary: Tied up with local police in Maharashtra and Bangalore to take in, rehabilitate, and rehome retired police dogs.

source.quote: The organization started by conducting awareness programs in schools, slums, residential societies and various communities to educate individuals about what to do when they see a stray animal in distress.

significant_events.summary: Conducting awareness programs in schools, slums, residential societies, and communities to educate about stray animals in distress and animal protection.

source.quote: 23 animals were PTS in 2020.

Finally, many animals, in this process, were found sick or injured and due to lack of people on ground - they were left unreported. So we at YODA set up a large scale search and rescue team to go out and identify such animals in pain to treat on site or admit depending upon situation.

data.original_text: Youth Organization in Defense of Animals (YODA), Mumbai was born with a vision to create awareness of the plight of stray and abandoned animals in India.
We mainly work with dogs and cats, we neuter and work on first aid cases, adoption, rescues, rehabilitation.
YODA is open 6 days a week for free of cost OPD for homeless animals.

YODA is known in the Mumbai region for providing a wide range of animal welfare activities like rescue, rehabilitation, rehoming, medical aid, background research for adopters, and medical emergencies when required.
The organization actively finds homes for stray dogs and pups and mass sterilizations stray animals in different zones across Mumbai.

The organization started by conducting awareness programs in schools, slums, residential societies and various communities to educate individuals about what to do when they see a stray animal in distress.
YODA's Mission is to develop a community that values and respects animals.
From educating youth to emergency services for stray animals, we now positively help and impact approx. 1000 animals each month across Maharashtra.

We encourage communities to choose adoption over purchase of animals, provide emergency mental and physical medical rehabilitation for animals in distress, educate urban, semi- urban and rural communities on the subject and work towards overall prevention of cruelty.

With your support, in 2020, we made immense progress in scaling up our sick and injured stray animal rehabilitation practice, rescued a large number of animals who were the victims of grave cruelty, and we scaled up our feeding program to cover over 5 Lakh meals for stray animals who were left starving on the road due to the pandemic.
YODA gets over 100 animals successfully adopted each month.
YODA Animal Welfare has been recognized at the Being Human Awards for its incredible work in rescue and rehabilitation.

name: Youth Organisation in Defence of Animals
